---
title: "{{< meta course_title >}}"
deck_short_title: "{{< meta decks.d03.short_title >}}"
subtitle: |
  <span class="deck-title">{{< meta decks.d03.title >}}</span><br>
  <span class="deck-meta">{{< meta texts.cb.short >}} Ch. 8<br> 
  </span>
---

{{< include ../classlib/classlib/quarto/slides/shared/statistics/hypothesis/hypothesis-frame.qmd >}}


{{< include ../classlib/classlib/quarto/slides/shared/statistics/hypothesis/hypothesis-examples.qmd >}}


{{< include ../classlib/classlib/quarto/slides/shared/statistics/hypothesis/likelihoodratio.qmd >}}

## Proof of the Neyman–Pearson lemma (simple vs simple)

Test
$$
H_0:\theta=\theta_0,
\qquad
H_A:\theta=\theta_1
$$

Let densities under these two hypotheses be $\varrho_0(x)$ and $\varrho_1(x)$

Among all tests with size $\alpha$,

::: {.key-point}
The likelihood ratio test is most powerful
:::

Reject $H_0$ when
$$
\frac{\varrho_1(x)}{\varrho_0(x)} > k
$$

where $k$ is chosen so that
$$
\Prob_{\theta_0}(\text{Reject}) = \alpha
$$

---
## Finishing the proof

Expand:

$$
\int (\phi^{\LRT} - \phi)\varrho_1\,dx
-
k
\int (\phi^{\LRT} - \phi)\varrho_0\,dx
\ge 0.
$$

But

$$
\int \phi \varrho_0\,dx
=
\Ex_{\theta_0}[\phi]
\le
\Ex_{\theta_0}[\phi^{\LRT}]
=
\int \phi^{\LRT} \varrho_0\,dx,
$$

so

$$
\int (\phi^{\LRT} - \phi)\varrho_0\,dx \ge 0.
$$

Therefore

$$
\int \phi^{\LRT} \varrho_1\,dx
\ge
\int \phi \varrho_1\,dx.
$$

Thus

$$
\power_{\theta_1}(\phi^{\LRT})
\ge
\power_{\theta_1}(\phi).
$$

$\square$
### Proof by comparison argument

Let $\phi^{\LRT}(x)$ be the likelihood ratio test
Let $\phi(x)$ be any other test with

$$
\Ex_{\theta_0}[\phi] \le \alpha
=
\Ex_{\theta_0}[\phi^{\LRT}].
$$

Consider

$$
\int (\phi^{\LRT} - \phi)(\varrho_1 - k \varrho_0)\,dx.
$$

By construction of $\phi^{\LRT}$:

- If $\phi^{\LRT}(x)=1$ and $\phi(x)=0$, then $\varrho_1 - k\varrho_0 \ge 0$  
- If $\phi^{\LRT}(x)=0$ and $\phi(x)=1$, then $\varrho_1 - k\varrho_0 \le 0$

Hence

$$
\int (\phi^{\LRT} - \phi)(\varrho_1 - k \varrho_0)\,dx \ge 0.
$$


## Finishing the proof

Expand:

$$
\int (\phi^{\LRT} - \phi)\varrho_1\,dx
-
k
\int (\phi^{\LRT} - \phi)\varrho_0\,dx
\ge 0.
$$

But

$$
\int \phi \varrho_0\,dx
=
\Ex_{\theta_0}[\phi]
\le
\Ex_{\theta_0}[\phi^{\LRT}]
=
\int \phi^{\LRT} \varrho_0\,dx,
$$

so

$$
\int (\phi^{\LRT} - \phi)\varrho_0\,dx \ge 0.
$$

Therefore

$$
\int \phi^{\LRT} \varrho_1\,dx
\ge
\int \phi \varrho_1\,dx.
$$

Thus

$$
\power_{\theta_1}(\phi^{\LRT})
\ge
\power_{\theta_1}(\phi).
$$

$\square$


```{=html}
<div class="deck-nav">
  <a class="deck-prev" href="{{< meta decks.d02.file >}}">← {{< meta decks.d02.title >}}</a>
  <span class="deck-next deck-disabled"></span>
</div>
```
